column3: A NIC provides a device's interface to a network.
column4:
Exam Trap

The NIC is the interface hardware; its MAC address identifies that interface for local data-link communication.
column8: Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.16; CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v2.0, Objective 2.5
column9: A+::220-1201::2.5 A+::220-1201::Domain2-Networking A+::220-1201::NetworkingHardwareDevices Cloze NIC Acronym Source::Messer-v170

column2: In a physical MAC address, MAC stands for {{c1::media access control}}.
column3: Switches use MAC addresses when forwarding Ethernet frames on a local network.
column4:
Exam Trap

Do not confuse the NIC's MAC address with its network-layer IP address.
column8: Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.16; CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v2.0, Objective 2.5
column9: A+::220-1201::2.5 A+::220-1201::Domain2-Networking A+::220-1201::NetworkingHardwareDevices Cloze HighYield MAC Acronym Source::Messer-v170

column3: PoE can carry electrical power and network data over an Ethernet connection.
column4:
Why

PoE can simplify installation where a separate nearby power outlet is inconvenient, but the source and powered device must support compatible standards.
column8: Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.16; CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v2.0, Objective 2.5
column9: A+::220-1201::2.5 A+::220-1201::Domain2-Networking A+::220-1201::NetworkingHardwareDevices Cloze PoE Acronym Source::Messer-v170

column3: An ONT terminates a provider's optical connection at the customer premises.
column4:
Exam Trap

The ONT is associated with the fiber handoff, not cable or DSL service.
column8: Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.16; CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v2.0, Objective 2.5
column9: A+::220-1201::2.5 A+::220-1201::Domain2-Networking A+::220-1201::NetworkingHardwareDevices Cloze ONT Acronym Source::Messer-v170
